{{nihongo|'''Kamo Shrine'''|鴨神社 (高槻市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]].

category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)}} [[JA:鴨神社 (高槻市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675604}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 鴨神社 |画像 = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) keidai.JPG|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|高槻市|Q335423}}赤大路町42-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|21.82|N|135|35|4.14|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鴨神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山積命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月29日|Q2535}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=鴨神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Kamo 001.jpg|thumb|200px|right|鳥居]] '''鴨神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、大阪府{{jalink|高槻市|Q335423}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 現在の祭神は以下の通り祭神は[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html 鴨神社由緒]（公式サイト）による。なお、大山積命は祭神の中でも同サイト内や境内の神社明細帳で別格視される。。 * '''{{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山積命}}''' （おおやまづみのみこと） * {{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}} （いざなぎのみこと） * {{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊弉冉尊}} （いざなみのみこと） * {{jalink|賀茂建角身命|Q11634939|鴨御祖大神}} （かものみおやおおかみ） 祭神について『神社明細帳』等の諸書では大山積神とされるが、後述のように当社を式内社・三島鴨神社とみる説では、祭神は鴨氏の祖神とみられる。また、当地は三島県主が勢力を持っていた所であることから、その祖神を祭神とみる説もある。なお『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』では、三島県主後裔の三島宿禰（右京神別）と賀茂県主（山城国神別）は同じ祖先と伝えている。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 創建は不詳。社伝では、先住の{{jalink|鴨氏}}一族が創祀し、のち渡来系の三島一族が勢力を伸ばしたため、後述の「三島鴨神社」とも称されたとする[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html 摂津・三島の元氏神]（公式サイト）。。 === 概史 === {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期の『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』には{{jalink|式内社}}として「摂津国{{jalink|島下郡|Q10976940}} 三島鴨神社」の記載がある。天坊幸彦が「三島郡の史跡と名勝」で、『大阪府神社明細帳』に「旧記も無御座候へども先前より申伝祭神大山祇命鎮座地所は故島下郡ト称シ（該村往古ハ島下郡ナリ徳川氏ノ代島上郡ヘ編入ノ由申伝）・・・三島鴨トモ俗ニ五位鴨社トモ又三島神トモ申伝来候云々」とあることから、延喜式の三島鴨神社はこの鴨神社であると指摘して以来、当社はその{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}の1つとなっている。 式内社の他の論社としては三島江に{{jalink|三島鴨神社|Q11355295}}がある。三島鴨神社は{{jalink|島上郡|Q10965808}}の位置にあたることから、郡域としては当社が有力視される『大阪府の地名』鴨神社項。。一方で、『伊予国風土記』逸文では大山積神が「津国の御島」から伊予に移ったと記すが、神の別称として「和多志（渡し）の神」と記すことから、河川から離れた鴨神社はこれに当たらないという指摘もある松下煌「三島鴨神社」（谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 3 摂津・河内・和泉・淡路』（{{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}}））。。いずれにしても、現在も特定には至っていない。 平安時代末期には、{{jalink|後白河天皇|Q317984}}中宮・{{jalink|建春門院}}が「三島神」に皇子誕生の祈願をしたと伝わる[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html 鴨神社由緒]（公式サイト）。。その結果のちの{{jalink|高倉天皇|Q357261}}が誕生し、これを祝して京都五条坂に三島神社が創建されたという。ただし院の参詣先は「三島神」であり、これについても三島江の三島鴨神社とみる説がある[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html 鴨神社だより]（公式サイト）。。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列した。{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}46年（{{jalink|1971年|Q2475}}）、本殿・拝殿が新築された。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}8年（{{jalink|884年|Q23895}}）12月21日、正六位上から従五位下 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「三島神」。ただし{{jalink|三島鴨神社|Q11355295}}とする説もある。 === 境内 === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|本殿 File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Kamo 003.jpg|稲荷神社　　 === 現地情報 === ; 所在地 * {{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|高槻市|Q335423}}赤大路町42-3 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}}{{jalink|JR京都線|Q1195820|京都線}} {{jalink|摂津富田駅|Q59513}} （西北へ徒歩13分） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === * 天坊幸彦「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961年）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』三島郡（1974年）収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） * 『日本歴史地名体系 大阪府の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）高槻市 鴨神社項 === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|三島鴨神社|Q11355295}} === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|鴨神社}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ 鴨神社]（公式サイト） * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html 鴨神社]（大阪府神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かもしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)}} [[JA:鴨神社 (高槻市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675604}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 鴨神社 |画像 = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) keidai.JPG|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|高槻市|Q335423}}赤大路町42-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|21.82|N|135|35|4.14|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=鴨神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山積命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{jalink|4月29日|Q2535}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=鴨神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Kamo 001.jpg|thumb|200px|right|鳥居]] '''鴨神社'''（かもじんじゃ）は、大阪府{{jalink|高槻市|Q335423}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 現在の祭神は以下の通り祭神は[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html 鴨神社由緒]（公式サイト）による。なお、大山積命は祭神の中でも同サイト内や境内の神社明細帳で別格視される。。 * '''{{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山積命}}''' （おおやまづみのみこと） * {{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊弉諾尊}} （いざなぎのみこと） * {{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊弉冉尊}} （いざなみのみこと） * {{jalink|賀茂建角身命|Q11634939|鴨御祖大神}} （かものみおやおおかみ） 祭神について『神社明細帳』等の諸書では大山積神とされるが、後述のように当社を式内社・三島鴨神社とみる説では、祭神は鴨氏の祖神とみられる。また、当地は三島県主が勢力を持っていた所であることから、その祖神を祭神とみる説もある。なお『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』では、三島県主後裔の三島宿禰（右京神別）と賀茂県主（山城国神別）は同じ祖先と伝えている。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 創建は不詳。社伝では、先住の{{jalink|鴨氏}}一族が創祀し、のち渡来系の三島一族が勢力を伸ばしたため、後述の「三島鴨神社」とも称されたとする[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html 摂津・三島の元氏神]（公式サイト）。。 === 概史 === {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期の『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』には{{jalink|式内社}}として「摂津国{{jalink|島下郡|Q10976940}} 三島鴨神社」の記載がある。天坊幸彦が「三島郡の史跡と名勝」で、『大阪府神社明細帳』に「旧記も無御座候へども先前より申伝祭神大山祇命鎮座地所は故島下郡ト称シ（該村往古ハ島下郡ナリ徳川氏ノ代島上郡ヘ編入ノ由申伝）・・・三島鴨トモ俗ニ五位鴨社トモ又三島神トモ申伝来候云々」とあることから、延喜式の三島鴨神社はこの鴨神社であると指摘して以来、当社はその{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}の1つとなっている。 式内社の他の論社としては三島江に{{jalink|三島鴨神社|Q11355295}}がある。三島鴨神社は{{jalink|島上郡|Q10965808}}の位置にあたることから、郡域としては当社が有力視される『大阪府の地名』鴨神社項。。一方で、『伊予国風土記』逸文では大山積神が「津国の御島」から伊予に移ったと記すが、神の別称として「和多志（渡し）の神」と記すことから、河川から離れた鴨神社はこれに当たらないという指摘もある松下煌「三島鴨神社」（谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 3 摂津・河内・和泉・淡路』（{{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}}））。。いずれにしても、現在も特定には至っていない。 平安時代末期には、{{jalink|後白河天皇|Q317984}}中宮・{{jalink|建春門院}}が「三島神」に皇子誕生の祈願をしたと伝わる[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html 鴨神社由緒]（公式サイト）。。その結果のちの{{jalink|高倉天皇|Q357261}}が誕生し、これを祝して京都五条坂に三島神社が創建されたという。ただし院の参詣先は「三島神」であり、これについても三島江の三島鴨神社とみる説がある[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html 鴨神社だより]（公式サイト）。。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列した。{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}46年（{{jalink|1971年|Q2475}}）、本殿・拝殿が新築された。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}8年（{{jalink|884年|Q23895}}）12月21日、正六位上から従五位下 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「三島神」。ただし{{jalink|三島鴨神社|Q11355295}}とする説もある。 === 境内 === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|本殿 File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Kamo 003.jpg|稲荷神社　　 === 現地情報 === ; 所在地 * {{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|高槻市|Q335423}}赤大路町42-3 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}}{{jalink|JR京都線|Q1195820|京都線}} {{jalink|摂津富田駅|Q59513}} （西北へ徒歩13分） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === * 天坊幸彦「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961年）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』三島郡（1974年）収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） * 『日本歴史地名体系 大阪府の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）高槻市 鴨神社項 === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|三島鴨神社|Q11355295}} === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|鴨神社}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ 鴨神社]（公式サイト） * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html 鴨神社]（大阪府神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かもしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] 鴨神社由緒]（公式サイト）。。その結果のちの{{jalink|高倉天皇|Q357261}}が誕生し、これを祝して京都五条坂に三島神社が創建されたという。ただし院の参詣先は「三島神」であり、これについても三島江の三島鴨神社とみる説がある[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html 鴨神社だより]（公式サイト）。。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列した。{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}46年（{{jalink|1971年|Q2475}}）、本殿・拝殿が新築された。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}8年（{{jalink|884年|Q23895}}）12月21日、正六位上から従五位下 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「三島神」。ただし{{jalink|三島鴨神社|Q11355295}}とする説もある。 === 境内 === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|本殿 File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Kamo 003.jpg|稲荷神社　　 === 現地情報 === ; 所在地 * {{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|高槻市|Q335423}}赤大路町42-3 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：{{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR西日本}}{{jalink|JR京都線|Q1195820|京都線}} {{jalink|摂津富田駅|Q59513}} （西北へ徒歩13分） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === * 天坊幸彦「三島郡の史跡と名勝」（1961年）（大阪府学務部編『大阪府史蹟名勝天然記念物』三島郡（1974年）収録　{{全国書誌番号|73009804}} ） * 『日本歴史地名体系 大阪府の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）高槻市 鴨神社項 === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|三島鴨神社|Q11355295}} === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|鴨神社}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ 鴨神社]（公式サイト） * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html 鴨神社]（大阪府神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かもしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)}} [[JA:鴨神社 (高槻市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675604}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |Image = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Takatsuki City|Q335423}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|21.82|N|135|35|4.14|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamo Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor) {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}}
Former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternative names = |Annual festival = {{jalink|April 29|Q2535}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Kamo 001.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Takatsuki City|Q335423}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}}, and under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsThe enshrined deities are based on [http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website). Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is treated as a deity of special status among them, according to the same site and the shrine register displayed within the precincts.. * '''{{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}''' * {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|Kamo no Mioya Ōkami}} Various documents such as the “Shrine Register” list the enshrined deity as Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami, but as described below, according to the theory that identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mishima Kamo Shrine, the enshrined deity is considered to be the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the “{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{jalink|Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{jalink|Shimakami District|Q10965808}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{jalink|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984}}’s consort {{jalink|Kenreimon-in}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{jalink|Emperor Takakura|Q357261}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, the shrine was designated as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. In {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 46 ({{jalink|1971|Q2475}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 8 ({{jalink|884|Q23895}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{jalink|Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Takatsuki City|Q335423}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|JR West}} {{jalink|JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|Kyoto Line}} {{jalink|Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{jalink|Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社|かもしんしや たかつきし]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]] [[Category:pages]]

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}}, and under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsThe enshrined deities are based on [http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website). Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is treated as a deity of special status among them, according to the same site and the shrine register displayed within the precincts.. * '''{{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}''' * {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|Kamo no Mioya Ōkami}} Various documents such as the “Shrine Register” list the enshrined deity as Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami, but as described below, according to the theory that identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mishima Kamo Shrine, the enshrined deity is considered to be the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the “{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{jalink|Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{jalink|Shimakami District|Q10965808}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{jalink|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984}}’s consort {{jalink|Kenreimon-in}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{jalink|Emperor Takakura|Q357261}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, the shrine was designated as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. In {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 46 ({{jalink|1971|Q2475}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 8 ({{jalink|884|Q23895}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{jalink|Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Takatsuki City|Q335423}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|JR West}} {{jalink|JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|Kyoto Line}} {{jalink|Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{jalink|Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社|かもしんしや たかつきし]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]] [[Category:pages]]

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}}, and under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsThe enshrined deities are based on [http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website). Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is treated as a deity of special status among them, according to the same site and the shrine register displayed within the precincts.. * '''{{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}''' * {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|Kamo no Mioya Ōkami}} Various documents such as the “Shrine Register” list the enshrined deity as Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami, but as described below, according to the theory that identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mishima Kamo Shrine, the enshrined deity is considered to be the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{jalink|Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295}} located in Mishimae. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{jalink|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984}}’s consort {{jalink|Kenreimon-in}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{jalink|Emperor Takakura|Q357261}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)}} [[JA:鴨神社 (高槻市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675604}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |Image = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Takatsuki City|Q335423}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|21.82|N|135|35|4.14|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamo Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor) {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}}
Former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternative names = |Annual festival = {{jalink|April 29|Q2535}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Kamo 001.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Takatsuki City|Q335423}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}}, and under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsThe enshrined deities are based on [http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website). Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is treated as a deity of special status among them, according to the same site and the shrine register displayed within the precincts.. * '''{{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}''' * {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|Kamo no Mioya Ōkami}} Various documents such as the “Shrine Register” list the enshrined deity as Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami, but as described below, according to the theory that identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mishima Kamo Shrine, the enshrined deity is considered to be the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the “{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{jalink|Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{jalink|Shimakami District|Q10965808}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{jalink|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984}}’s consort {{jalink|Kenreimon-in}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{jalink|Emperor Takakura|Q357261}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, the shrine was designated as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. In {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 46 ({{jalink|1971|Q2475}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 8 ({{jalink|884|Q23895}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{jalink|Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Takatsuki City|Q335423}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|JR West}} {{jalink|JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|Kyoto Line}} {{jalink|Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{jalink|Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社|かもしんしや たかつきし]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]] [[Category:pages]]

category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)}} [[JA:鴨神社 (高槻市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675604}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |Image = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Takatsuki City|Q335423}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|21.82|N|135|35|4.14|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamo Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor) {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}}
Former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |Alternative names = |Annual festival = {{jalink|April 29|Q2535}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Kamo 001.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Takatsuki City|Q335423}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}}, and under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|former shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsThe enshrined deities are based on [http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website). Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is treated as a deity of special status among them, according to the same site and the shrine register displayed within the precincts.. * '''{{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}''' * {{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|Kamo no Mioya Ōkami}} Various documents such as the “Shrine Register” list the enshrined deity as Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami, but as described below, according to the theory that identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mishima Kamo Shrine, the enshrined deity is considered to be the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}, the “{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{jalink|candidate shrine|Q135022904}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{jalink|Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{jalink|Shimakami District|Q10965808}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{jalink|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984}}’s consort {{jalink|Kenreimon-in}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{jalink|Emperor Takakura|Q357261}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, the shrine was designated as a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. In {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 46 ({{jalink|1971|Q2475}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 8 ({{jalink|884|Q23895}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{jalink|Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Takatsuki City|Q335423}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|JR West}} {{jalink|JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|Kyoto Line}} {{jalink|Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{jalink|Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社|かもしんしや たかつきし]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]] [[Category:pages]]

As a result, the future {{ill|lt=Emperor Takakura|Q357261|WD=Q357261}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 46 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|lt=Kyoto Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513|WD=Q59513}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社|かもしんしや たかつきし]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]] [[Category:pages]]

category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)}} [[JA:鴨神社 (高槻市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675604}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |Image = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|21.82|N|135|35|4.14|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamo Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (minor) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternative names = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Kamo 001.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsThe enshrined deities are based on [http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website). Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is treated as a deity of special status among them, according to the same site and the shrine register displayed within the precincts.. * '''{{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}''' * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamo no Mioya Ōkami}} Various documents such as the “Shrine Register” list the enshrined deity as Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami, but as described below, according to the theory that identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mishima Kamo Shrine, the enshrined deity is considered to be the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984|WD=Q317984}}’s consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kenreimon-in|UNKNOWN}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{ill|lt=Emperor Takakura|Q357261|WD=Q357261}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 46 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|lt=Kyoto Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513|WD=Q59513}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社|かもしんしや たかつきし]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]] [[Category:pages]]

Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternative names = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Kamo 001.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsThe enshrined deities are based on [http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website). Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is treated as a deity of special status among them, according to the same site and the shrine register displayed within the precincts.. * '''{{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}''' * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamo no Mioya Ōkami}} Various documents such as the “Shrine Register” list the enshrined deity as Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami, but as described below, according to the theory that identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mishima Kamo Shrine, the enshrined deity is considered to be the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984|WD=Q317984}}’s consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kenreimon-in|1=UNKNOWN}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{ill|lt=Emperor Takakura|Q357261|WD=Q357261}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 46 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|lt=Kyoto Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513|WD=Q59513}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社|かもしんしや たかつきし]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]] [[Category:pages]]

category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)}} [[JA:鴨神社 (高槻市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675604}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |Image = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|21.82|N|135|35|4.14|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamo Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternative names = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Kamo 001.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsThe enshrined deities are based on [http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website). Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is treated as a deity of special status among them, according to the same site and the shrine register displayed within the precincts.. * '''{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto}}''' * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamo no Mioya Ōkami}} Various documents such as the “Shrine Register” list the enshrined deity as Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami, but as described below, according to the theory that identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mishima Kamo Shrine, the enshrined deity is considered to be the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984|WD=Q317984}}’s consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kenreimon-in|1=UNKNOWN}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{ill|lt=Emperor Takakura|Q357261|WD=Q357261}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 46 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|lt=Kyoto Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513|WD=Q59513}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社|かもしんしや たかつきし]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]] [[Category:pages]]

According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, the “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984|WD=Q317984}}’s consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kenreimon-in|1=UNKNOWN}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{ill|lt=Emperor Takakura|Q357261|WD=Q357261}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 46 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|lt=Kyoto Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513|WD=Q59513}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社|かもしんしや たかつきし]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]] [[Category:pages]]
