Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative names = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Kamo 001.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsThe enshrined deities are based on [http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website). Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is treated as a deity of special status among them, according to the same site and the shrine register displayed within the precincts.. * '''{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} * {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamo no Mioya Ōkami|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} Various documents such as the “Shrine Register” list the enshrined deity as Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami, but as described below, according to the theory that identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mishima Kamo Shrine, the enshrined deity is considered to be the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808|1=Shimakami district}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984|WD=Q317984|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa}}’s consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kenreimon-in|1=UNKNOWN}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{ill|lt=Emperor Takakura|Q357261|WD=Q357261|1=Emperor Takakura}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 46 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|lt=Kyoto Line|1=JR Kyōto Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513|WD=Q59513|1=Settsu-Tonda Station}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社|かもしんしや たかつきし]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]] [[Category:pages]]

(Takatsuki) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|21.82|N|135|35|4.14|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamo Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative names = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Kamo 001.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsThe enshrined deities are based on [http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website). Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is treated as a deity of special status among them, according to the same site and the shrine register displayed within the precincts.. * '''{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} * {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamo no Mioya Ōkami|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} Various documents such as the “Shrine Register” list the enshrined deity as Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami, but as described below, according to the theory that identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mishima Kamo Shrine, the enshrined deity is considered to be the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808|1=Shimakami district}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984|WD=Q317984|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa}}’s consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kenreimon-in|1=UNKNOWN}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{ill|lt=Emperor Takakura|Q357261|WD=Q357261|1=Emperor Takakura}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 46 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|lt=Kyoto Line|1=JR Kyōto Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513|WD=Q59513|1=Settsu-Tonda Station}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kamojinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社|かもしんしや たかつきし]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]] [[Category:pages]]

In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808|1=Shimakami district}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984|WD=Q317984|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa}}’s consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kenreimon-in|1=UNKNOWN}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{ill|lt=Emperor Takakura|Q357261|WD=Q357261|1=Emperor Takakura}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 46 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|lt=Kyoto Line|1=JR Kyōto Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513|WD=Q59513|1=Settsu-Tonda Station}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社|かもしんしや たかつきし]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]] [[Category:pages]]

Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative names = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Kamo 001.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsThe enshrined deities are based on [http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website). Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is treated as a deity of special status among them, according to the same site and the shrine register displayed within the precincts.. * '''{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} * {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamo no Mioya Ōkami|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} Various documents such as the “Shrine Register” list the enshrined deity as Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami, but as described below, according to the theory that identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mishima Kamo Shrine, the enshrined deity is considered to be the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808|1=Shimakami district}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984|WD=Q317984|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa}}’s consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kenreimon-in|1=UNKNOWN}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{ill|lt=Emperor Takakura|Q357261|WD=Q357261|1=Emperor Takakura}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 46 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|lt=Kyoto Line|1=JR Kyōto Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513|WD=Q59513|1=Settsu-Tonda Station}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:加茂神社|かもしんしや たかつきし]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:pages]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)}} [[JA:鴨神社 (高槻市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675604}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |Image = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|21.82|N|135|35|4.14|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamo Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative names = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Kamo 001.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsThe enshrined deities are based on [http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website). Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is treated as a deity of special status among them, according to the same site and the shrine register displayed within the precincts.. * '''{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} * {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamo no Mioya Ōkami|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} Various documents such as the “Shrine Register” list the enshrined deity as Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami, but as described below, according to the theory that identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mishima Kamo Shrine, the enshrined deity is considered to be the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808|1=Shimakami district}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984|WD=Q317984|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa}}’s consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kenreimon-in|1=UNKNOWN}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{ill|lt=Emperor Takakura|Q357261|WD=Q357261|1=Emperor Takakura}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 46 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|lt=Kyoto Line|1=JR Kyōto Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513|WD=Q59513|1=Settsu-Tonda Station}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:pages]]

Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative names = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Kamo 001.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsThe enshrined deities are based on [http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website). Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is treated as a deity of special status among them, according to the same site and the shrine register displayed within the precincts.. * '''{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} * {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamo no Mioya Ōkami|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} Various documents such as the “Shrine Register” list the enshrined deity as Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami, but as described below, according to the theory that identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mishima Kamo Shrine, the enshrined deity is considered to be the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808|1=Shimakami district}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984|WD=Q317984|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa}}’s consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kenreimon-in|1=UNKNOWN}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{ill|lt=Emperor Takakura|Q357261|WD=Q357261|1=Emperor Takakura}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 46 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|lt=Kyoto Line|1=JR Kyōto Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513|WD=Q59513|1=Settsu-Tonda Station}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:pages]]

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 46 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|lt=Kyoto Line|1=JR Kyōto Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513|WD=Q59513|1=Settsu-Tonda Station}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:pages]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)}} [[JA:鴨神社 (高槻市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675604}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |Image = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|21.82|N|135|35|4.14|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamo Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative names = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Kamo 001.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsThe enshrined deities are based on [http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website). Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is treated as a deity of special status among them, according to the same site and the shrine register displayed within the precincts.. * '''{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} * {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamo no Mioya Ōkami|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} Various documents such as the “Shrine Register” list the enshrined deity as Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami, but as described below, according to the theory that identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mishima Kamo Shrine, the enshrined deity is considered to be the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808|1=Shimakami district}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984|WD=Q317984|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa}}’s consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kenreimon-in|1=UNKNOWN}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{ill|lt=Emperor Takakura|Q357261|WD=Q357261|1=Emperor Takakura}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 46 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|lt=Kyoto Line|1=JR Kyōto Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513|WD=Q59513|1=Settsu-Tonda Station}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:pages]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)}} [[JA:鴨神社 (高槻市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11675604}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kamo Shrine |Image = [[File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|21.82|N|135|35|4.14|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamo Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative names = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Kamo 001.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are as followsThe enshrined deities are based on [http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website). Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto is treated as a deity of special status among them, according to the same site and the shrine register displayed within the precincts.. * '''{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}''' * {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} * {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi-no-Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Kamo no Mioya Ōkami|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}} Various documents such as the “Shrine Register” list the enshrined deity as Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami, but as described below, according to the theory that identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha Mishima Kamo Shrine, the enshrined deity is considered to be the ancestral deity of the Kamo clan. In addition, since this area was a place where the Mishima Agatanushi held power, there is also a theory that regards their ancestral deity as the enshrined deity. The “{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}” records that the Mishima no Sukune (descendants of the Mishima Agatanushi, Jingibetsu of the Right Capital) and the Kamo Agatanushi (Jingibetsu of Yamashiro Province) share the same ancestor. === History === === Foundation === The date of foundation is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was originally established by the resident Kamo clan, and later came to be known as “Mishima Kamo Shrine” because the immigrant Mishima clan rose to power in the area[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja03.html The original tutelary deity of Settsu–Mishima] (official website).. === Historical overview === In the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, the “{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}” lists “Mishima Kamo Shrine, Shimashimo District, Settsu Province” as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Since Yukihiko Ambo pointed out in “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” that the “Osaka Prefecture Shrine Register” states: “Although there are no old records, it has long been handed down that the enshrined deity is Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto; the place of enshrinement was formerly called Shimashimo District (this village was originally in Shimashimo District and was incorporated into Shimakami District during the Tokugawa era) … it has also been popularly called Mishima Kamo, Go-i Kamo Shrine, or Mishima Shrine,” this shrine has been regarded as one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the Engishiki Mishima Kamo Shrine. Another candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha is {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} located in Mishimae. Since Mishima Kamo Shrine lies within {{ill|lt=Shimakami District|Q10965808|WD=Q10965808|1=Shimakami district}}, this shrine is considered more plausible in terms of district boundaries“Place Names of Osaka Prefecture,” entry on Kamo Shrine.. On the other hand, an excerpt from the “Iyo Province Fudoki” records that Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami moved from “Mishima of Tsukushi Province” to Iyo, and since the deity is also called the “God of the Crossing,” it has been argued that Kamo Shrine, which is distant from rivers, does not fit this descriptionMatsushita Akira, “Mishima Kamo Shrine,” in Ken’ichi Tanigawa (ed.), “Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Vol. 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji” ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}).. In any case, no definitive conclusion has been reached. At the end of the Heian period, it is said that {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Q317984|WD=Q317984|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa}}’s consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kenreimon-in|1=UNKNOWN}} prayed at “Mishima Shrine” for the birth of a prince[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/kamojinja01.html History of Kamo Shrine] (official website).. As a result, the future {{ill|lt=Emperor Takakura|Q357261|WD=Q357261|1=Emperor Takakura}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 46 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|lt=Kyoto Line|1=JR Kyōto Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513|WD=Q59513|1=Settsu-Tonda Station}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] [[Category:pages]]

As a result, the future {{ill|lt=Emperor Takakura|Q357261|WD=Q357261|1=Emperor Takakura}} was born, and to celebrate this, Mishima Shrine was founded at Gojozaka in Kyoto. However, the place of her worship is recorded as “Mishima Shrine,” and there is also a theory that this refers to Mishima Kamo Shrine at Mishimae[http://kamojinja.web.fc2.com/120504-02.html Kamo Shrine Newsletter] (official website).. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 46 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}), the main hall and worship hall were newly constructed. === Court rank === * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), December 21: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (“{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}”) – recorded as “Mishima Shrine.” There is also a theory that this refers to {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}}. === Shrine precincts === File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki) haiden.JPG|Worship hall File:Kamo 003.jpg|Inari Shrine　　 === On-site information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki City|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} 42-3 Akaōji-chō ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|WD=Q1195820|lt=Kyoto Line|1=JR Kyōto Line}} {{ill|lt=Settsu-Tonda Station|Q59513|WD=Q59513|1=Settsu-Tonda Station}} (13-minute walk northwest) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Yukihiko Ambo, “Historical Sites and Scenic Places of Mishima District” (1961), included in Osaka Prefectural Board of Education (ed.), “Historical Sites, Scenic Places, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture: Mishima District” (1974) {{National Bibliography Number|73009804}} * “Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names: Osaka Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Takatsuki City, entry on Kamo Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Kamo Shrine|Q11355295|WD=Q11355295|1=Mishima-Kamo Shrine}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Takatsuki)|Kamo Shrine}} * [https://kamojinja.amebaownd.com/ Kamo Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai2sibu/takatuki-city/m08_29_takatuki_kamo.html Kamo Shrine] (Osaka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Q11675604)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]
